items.title: Amazon to invest over US$33bil in Southeast Asia cloud, AI infrastructure by 2039
items.summary: SINGAPORE: Amazon said here on Thursday it plans to invest more than US$33 billion in cloud computing and artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure across Indonesia, Malaysia, Singapore and Thailand by 2039, underscoring the region's growing role in global digital expansion, reported Xinhua. It said it is expanding cloud and AI infrastructure across the region to support businesses and organisations that are increasingly adopting digital technologies. David Zapolsky, chief global affairs and legal officer at Amazon, said that governments in Southeast Asia have played a key role in fostering policies and economic conditions that are accelerating investment in AI and digital infrastructure at scale.

items.title: Police record Tony Pua’s statement over Facebook post on monarchy, Rukun Negara
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 21 — Police recorded former Damansara MP Tony Pua’s statement today over his Facebook post that allegedly touched on sensitive issues involving the constitutional monarchy and Rukun Negara. Bukit Aman Criminal Investigations Department (CID) director Datuk M Kumar said the Selangor CID was still taking Tony Pua’s statement at the Selangor contingent police headquarters. The statement is believed to have been made following the Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah’s, decree advising Seri Kembangan state assemblyman Wong Siew Ki and former Selangor executive councillor Ronnie Liu to visit Dataran Selangor once a week to understand and appreciate the true meaning of the Rukun Negara.

items.title: Genting's Q1 net profit jumps to RM101.1mil vs RM4.6mil a year ago
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Genting Bhd's net profit for the first quarter (Q1) ended March 31, 2026, jumped to RM101.10 million from RM4.60 million in the same quarter a year ago. Genting said that Resorts World Sentosa (RWS) recorded revenue of RM1.88 billion in the current quarter under review. As for its oil palm plantation segment, Genting said revenue was higher in the current quarter, and additionally, the previous year's corresponding quarter featured higher profit realised on brought-forward inventory.

items.title: MNRB FY26 profit soars to RM545.39mil, sets new earnings benchmark
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: MNRB Holdings Bhd's net profit rose to RM545.39 million for the financial year ended March 31, 2026 (FY26) from RM394.25 million in the preceding year. "The General Takaful business delivered an impressive performance, with total revenue increasing by 31.8 per cent, or RM291.7 million, to RM1.20 billion from RM916.9 million in the prior year," it said. MNRB said its Family Takaful business also continued its upward trajectory, posting total revenue of RM730.3 million, an increase of RM137.8 million, or 23.3 per cent, from RM592.5 million.

items.title: Ni Hsin unit to assemble, distribute surron electric off-road motorcycles in Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Ni Hsin Group Bhd's wholly owned subsidiary, Ni Hsin EV Tech Sdn Bhd (NHEVT) has signed the Motorcycle Completely Knocked Down (CKD) Assembly Cooperation Agreement with Chongqing Qiulong Technology Co Ltd (Qiulong) to assemble and sell the SURRON brand of electric off-road motorcycle (dirt bike) in Malaysia. "The partnership enables Qiulong to expand its market reach to Malaysia, while maintaining a capital-efficient structure," it said. Ni Hsin said the alliance combines NHEVT's capabilities as an experienced electric vehicles (EV) OEM assembler with Qiulong's expertise in producing high-performance new energy personal transportation tools, supported by its strong research and development (R&D) and design capabilities, to expand Qiulong's market reach in the region.

items.title: Coastal Contracts secures US$92.5mil contract from Mexico's Pemex
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Energy infrastructure and marine services and solutions provider Coastal Contracts Bhd's consortium has entered into a US$92.5 million (US$1 = RM3.97) contract with Mexican state-owned petroleum corporation Petroleos Mexicanos (Pemex) on May 20, 2026. In a Bursa Malaysia filing, Coastal Contracts said the contract, awarded in accordance with Pemex's emergency exception process, involved the design, engineering, transport, installation, interconnection, commissioning, testing and startup of the Permanent Primary Infrastructure of the second Perdiz Plant at the Ixachi Field, Mexico. "Following the successful completion of the project within the stipulated timeline, the plant has now commenced operations.

items.title: MOT defends single‑sourcing RFP for Penang Mutiara Line LRT, cites legal and technical reasons
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 21 — Every decision regarding the Penang Mutiara Line LRT project is made based on careful technical, legal and fiscal considerations to ensure that the people of the state have access to the public transport system. This project was originally a Penang State Government project under the Penang Transport Master Plan (PTMP). To implement PTMP, MOT announced that the State Government appointed SRS Consortium Sdn Bhd as the Project Delivery Partner (PDP) through an open tender in August 2015 In March 2024, the Federal Government agreed to finance and fully take over the Mutiara Line LRT from the State Government on the condition that the reclamation project be limited to one island only, following Cabinet approval, with MRT Corp appointed as the developer and asset owner of the project.

items.title: Petron Malaysia begins limited refinery operations amid Port Dickson jetty repairs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Petron Malaysia Refining and Marketing Bhd has started limited and intermittent refinery operations at its refinery in Port Dickson, Negeri Sembilan, to process existing in-tank crude inventories in a move aimed at supporting product availability amid the ongoing Middle East crisis. In a filing with Bursa Malaysia today, Petron Malaysia said it is continuing efforts to address operational constraints, including securing crude supply to support limited and stable refinery operations while awaiting the construction of a replacement jetty. The company noted that debris-clearing works involving the sunken product jetty are progressing, while major contracts for the construction of the new jetty have recently been awarded.

items.title: TH Plantations' net profit eases to RM11.62mil in Q1 2026
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: TH Plantations Bhd's net profit eased by 9.49 per cent to RM11.62 million in the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 (1Q 2026) compared with RM12.84 million in the same period last year. Revenue, however, increased by 3.33 per cent to RM189.48 million from RM183.38 million previously, it said in a filing with Bursa Malaysia. In a separate statement, TH Plantations said during this quarter, yield increased by 16 per cent, while oil extraction rate and kernel extraction rate improved by six per cent and 13 per cent, respectively, compared to the same period last year, reflecting stronger operational performance across the group's estates and mills.

items.title: Diesel price hikes push up building material costs by up to 30pct, say contractors
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The increase in diesel prices has pushed up building material costs by 20 to 30 per cent, said the Malaysian Housing Contractors Association. Association president Dr Muhsin Abdul Ghani said the situation has also led to construction costs rising by around 10 to 15 per cent compared with before, causing some customers to take a more cautious approach, including postponing plans to build homes. "Rising construction costs have also pushed up house prices by around 10 to 15 per cent compared with before.

items.title: Ringgit closes higher against US dollar, regional peers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit closed higher against the US dollar today, supported by Malaysia's favourable domestic fundamentals. At 6pm, the ringgit appreciated to 3.9595/9630 versus the greenback from 3.9675/9715 at Wednesday's close. At the close, the ringgit traded mostly lower against a basket of major currencies.

items.title: Johor businessman claims loan sharks threatened children’s lives after alleged RM50,000 loan scam
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 21 — A 36-year-old businessman is now living in fear after several moneylenders threatened to kidnap and sell his son’s organs, in addition to injuring him and burning down his house over a loan application. Chan Chee Hau said he received the threat shortly after he applied to take out a RM50,000 loan meant for working capital for his car rental business in Kuala Lumpur. His ordeal started on May 8 after he saw a loan advertisement on Facebook that used a licensed loan company logo.

items.title: Jerantut family may still wait for lights despite electricity breakthrough
items.summary: Muhammad Ramadhan Abdul Hamid, 30, said indoor wiring from the main switch box has yet to be completed, and no lighting equipment has been purchased. "I am extremely grateful to Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB) for beginning installation works on the utility poles, with electricity supply targeted to start this Sunday. Works are ongoing, with electricity supply targeted to begin on May 24.

items.title: Majuperak's exit from affected listed issuer status signals recovery
items.summary: IPOH: The announcement by Bursa Malaysia regarding the removal of the Affected Listed Issuer status on Majuperak Holdings Bhd marks a significant milestone in the company's recovery journey. In a statement, Perbadanan Kemajuan Negeri Perak (PKNPk) said the status removal, which takes effect today, reflects the effectiveness of the restructuring and strengthening measures undertaken by the state-owned company's management over the past few years. "Throughout the recovery period, Majuperak implemented various improvement initiatives in a disciplined and responsible manner, including strengthening operational management, enhancing corporate compliance and streamlining business strategies to ensure the company's long-term sustainability," the statement said.

items.title: TM posts lower Q1 profit of RM321.51mil, hit by one-off 5G write-down
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Telekom Malaysia Bhd (TM) posted a 19 per cent lower net profit to RM321.51 million for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 (1QFY26) from RM401.26 million a year ago. Revenue for the quarter rose 2.9 per cent year-on-year to RM2.93 billion, driven mainly by stronger contributions from its consumer and carrier-to-carrier businesses. Excluding one-off items, TM's underlying profit after tax and non-controlling interests (Patami) climbed 9.3 per cent to RM436.3 million, while underlying earnings before interest and tax (Ebit) increased 6.3 per cent to RM593.3 million.

items.title: Viral video shows child allegedly assaulted by babysitter in Seremban
items.summary: SEREMBAN: A disturbing video has gone viral showing a child left outside at the rear of a house and allegedly being assaulted by a woman believed to be the child's babysitter. Seremban district police chief Assistant Commissioner Azahar Abdul Rahim said police have urged the owner of the video to come forward and assist investigations. "Police have detected the viral video and are verifying the incident.

items.title: MARKET PULSE PM MAY 21, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia closed lower on Thursday, closing seven points lower at 1,710. EI Power, SkyeChip and Zetrix AI were among the top active at closing bell. That wraps up today's Market Pulse.

items.title: IRB hunts 2,213 tax defaulters in nationwide sweep
items.summary: The Inland Revenue Board (IRB) said in a statement today that the nationwide crackdown, targeting high-value tax arrears and compounds, is being carried out from May 21 to May 24. It said the TOG is a strategic measure to boost enforcement effectiveness against taxpayers who are recalcitrant in settling their tax arrears and compounds. "Among the main objectives of the TOG Series 1/2026 are to increase the collection of tax arrears and compounds more effectively, reinforce voluntary compliance among taxpayers, and boost enforcement effectiveness through collaborative networks with enforcement agencies," it said.

items.title: Genting Malaysia posts net loss of RM3.8mil in Q1
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Genting Malaysia Bhd posted a loss of RM3.8 million in the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 (Q1 FY26) from a profit of RM72.7 million a year ago due to unrealised foreign exchange gains. In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the company had lower net unrealised foreign exchange translation gains of RM14.6 million in Q1 FY26 versus RM50.4 million in the same period last year. "The group carried higher operating and payroll costs as a result of increases due to contracted union agreements and increases in national minimum wages.

items.title: Inside the unravelling of US diplomacy under Trump
items.summary: WHEN Donald Trump warned Iran on April 7 that "a whole civilisation will die tonight," a European diplomat in Washington said his government wanted an urgent answer to a chilling question: Was the US president contemplating the use of a nuclear weapon? Across Europe and Asia, the concern went beyond whether Trump's apocalyptic threat was real or bluster. European governments immediately sought reassurance through a traditional channel: the US State Department.

items.title: Kelantan mulls jail term for illegal gold miners
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Kelantan is studying the possibility of imposing harsher penalties, including jail sentences, against those involved in illegal gold mining activities. Deputy Menteri Besar Datuk Dr Mohamed Fadzli Hassan said the current punishment was not harsh enough on offenders, as the fines could easily be paid using the lucrative proceeds gained from illegal gold mining activities. "The area in question was not gazetted as a gold mining site.

items.title: Loan sharks threaten to burn down house, harvest children's organs
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: A businessman's attempt to secure an RM50,000 loan ended in a loss of RM1,329 and escalated into terrifying threats from loan sharks, including threats to burn down his house and harvest his children's organs. Chan Chee Hau claimed he initially received RM3,000, which was immediately banked into his account. The 36-year-old said the legal money lender had promised to return the RM4,000 along with the loan amount.

items.title: Jakoa denies restricting entry of outsiders into Orang Asli villages
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Department of Orang Asli Development (Jakoa) has denied allegations that it restricts the entry of outsiders into Orang Asli villages, noting that 395 applications were approved last year and 255 applications had been approved as of May this year. In a statement today, Jakoa said it remained committed to practising inclusive and open governance through strategic cooperation with various stakeholders, including non-governmental organisations (NGOs), the corporate sector, government agencies and academic institutions. It said 183 corporate social responsibility (CSR) programmes and 72 academic visits had been approved so far this year, while only a small number of applications were rejected due to the lack of consent from Tok Batin or Penghulu institutions.

items.title: TNB advances energy transition agenda, delivers long-term value to nation
items.summary: As Malaysia accelerates efforts under the National Energy Transition Roadmap (NETR), TNB continues to play a central role in supporting the country's aspirations towards a sustainable, inclusive and low-carbon energy future. Accelerating Malaysia's Energy Transition Throughout 2025, the group intensified efforts in renewable energy integration, grid digitalisation and carbon management to strengthen national energy security and enable economic growth. "As the country's leading utility provider, TNB plays an important role in supporting Malaysia's energy transition journey that is balanced, responsible and inclusive.

items.title: Sons laud security guard dad in San Diego who saved children from mosque attackers
items.summary: SAN DIEGO: The sons of a security guard killed while protecting children at the Islamic Center of San Diego remembered their father as a hero who died as he lived - putting others first. Police say Amin Abdullah, 51, a security guard at the largest mosque in San Diego County, sacrificed his life to keep 140 children inside the mosque school safe by engaging two gunmen in a shootout and transmitting the radio call that activated a security lockdown. I was just in shock," Muhammad Amin Abdullah, 28, said on Wednesday.

items.title: Trio convicted of trafficking to stay behind bars for 30-years
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Court of Appeal here has upheld the 30 years' jail sentence against three men convicted of trafficking 4kg of ketamine in 2020. The three-judge panel led by Datuk Noorin Badaruddin, together with Justices Datuk Mohd Radzi Abdul Hamid and Dean Wayne Daly, unanimously dismissed the appeals by Ha How Kok, 27, Hwong Nai Chong, 30, and Yeap Hock Guan, 52, against both their convictions and sentences. The court also upheld the 12 strokes of the rotan imposed on Ha and Hwong.

items.title: 17 passengers escape unhurt after express bus catches fire
items.summary: ALOR GAJAH: Seventeen passengers were unhurt after the express bus they were on caught fire after a suspected crash at Kilometre 195.9 southbound of the North-South Expressway here today. Melaka Fire and Rescue Department Public Relations Officer Mohd Hafidzatullah Rashid said they received a distress call about the fire at 3.04pm. "A team from the Alor Gajah Fire and Rescue Station was dispatched to the location along with a water tanker from the Ayer Keroh Fire and Rescue Station.

items.title: Two Ducati superbike riders charged after viral wheelie stunt along Setiawangsa‑Pantai Expressway
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 21 — Two high-powered motorcycle riders pleaded not guilty in the Magistrates’ Court here today to charges of performing dangerous ‘wheelie’ stunts on the Setiawangsa-Pantai Expressway (SPE) early this month. Khairul Azham Mohd Noor, 36, who works as a clerk, and Nashril Rosli, 31, a mechanic, made the plea after the charges against them were read out separately before Magistrate Tg Syazwany Yasmyn Tuan Roslan. Earlier, Prosecuting Officer Inspector Shazwan Hasifi Shahrun offered bail at RM2,000 for Nashril and RM3,000 for Khairul Azham, respectively, with one surety.

items.title: Injunction against Mahmud Abu Bekir stays in Cahya Mata Sarawak dispute
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court in Kuching has dismissed a preliminary objection raised by Datuk Seri Mahmud Abu Bekir Taib against an interim injunction obtained in an ongoing injunction dispute involving Cahya Mata Sarawak Berhad and nine other parties. The legal dispute is linked to boardroom and derivative action proceedings involving the company. Cahya Mata had obtained the injunction to restrain Mahmud from filing or continuing a proposed derivative action against the company.

items.title: Petron slips with Q1 net loss of RM35mil from RM81mil profit a year ago
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Petron Malaysia Refining and Marketing Bhd has posted a net loss of RM35.04 million in the first quarter of 2026 (Q1 FY26), a sharp reversal from a net profit of RM81 million in the same period last year. Domestically, Petron said retail sales volume grew four per cent reaching 4.4 million barrels during the first quarter of 2026 (Q1 FY26) compared to the same period last year. However, total sales volume fell 25 per cent to 6.9 million barrels from last year's 9.2 million barrels in the absence of exports due to refinery shutdown.

items.title: Zara Qairina's school performance unsatisfactory, court told
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The court heard that the late Zara Qairina Mahathir's academic performance was unsatisfactory, based on her school history and diary entries. Consultant forensic psychiatrist Dr Chua Sze Hung said the then 13-year-old's academic performance was not good, as she mentioned Arabic language in one of her diaries. Dr Chua also said that based on the conversation, he did not think the mother was giving any unhealthy pressure when it comes to academic performance.

items.title: 2 motorcyclists plead not guilty over wheelie stunts on expressway
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Two motorcyclists pleaded not guilty at the magistrate's court to charges of performing hazardous "wheelie" stunts over 80m along the Setiawangsa-Pantai Expressway (SPE) earlier this month. Prosecuting officer Inspector Shazwan Hasifi Shahrun proposed bail at RM2,000 for Nashril and RM3,000 for Khairul Azham, with one surety each. The court fixed July 8 for mention in Nashril's case and July 10 for Khairul Azham's.

items.title: Police probe TikTok account over posts insulting King
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have opened an investigation paper into a social media post deemed defamatory and insulting towards the Yang di-Pertuan Agong, Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar, posted on a TikTok account. Federal Criminal Investigation Department director Datuk M. Kumar confirmed the matter today after the Johor Royal Family Media and Communications Office, also known as the Royal Press Office, detected the content. "Yes, we have opened an investigation paper and are conducting further investigations through the Classified Criminal Investigation Unit (USJT) under the Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department (CID).

items.title: Indonesia's Aceh flogs four for sex outside marriage
items.summary: JAKARTA: Islamic officials in Indonesia's staunchly syariah Aceh province publicly flogged two women and two men on Thursday for having sex outside of marriage, an AFP reporter witnessed. "The women are sex workers, and the men are their customers," Muhammad Rizal, the head of Aceh's Islamic police force, told AFP. Caning, though not a legal form of punishment in Indonesia, retains strong support in Aceh to punish a range of offences that also include gambling and gay sex.

items.title: Children’s home caregiver gets 11 years behind bars, five strokes for abusing two young boys
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 21 — A caregiver at a children’s welfare home was sentenced by the Ampang Sessions Court today to 11 years’ imprisonment and five strokes of the cane after pleading guilty to six charges involving sexual offences and unnatural sex against two boys earlier this year. The court imposed one stroke of the cane for each of the first five charges, while no whipping sentence was imposed for the offence under Section 15(a) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017. In addition, the judge ordered the accused to undergo counselling throughout his imprisonment and placed him under police supervision for two years upon completion of his sentence.

items.title: Police probe sudden death after body of man found in drain near George Town shophouses
items.summary: The New Straits Times reported that passersby alerted police at 9.26am after discovering the man unconscious in the drain. North-east district deputy police chief Superintendent Lee Swee Sake said initial checks identified the deceased as a local man from Kapar, Selangor. He said police believed the man had been homeless and staying in the area where he was found.

items.title: Police arrest eight youths after teasing escalates into fight outside Klang secondary school
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 21 — Police arrested eight local men, including school students, believed to have been involved in a fight near a national secondary school in Klang on May 19. Klang Selatan District Police Chief ACP Lim Jit Huey said all the suspects, aged between 14 and 24, were detained by a police team from the Criminal Investigation Division of the Klang Selatan District Police Headquarters (IPD) immediately after receiving a report regarding the incident at 1.00am yesterday. “Preliminary investigations revealed that some of those arrested are still school students, while the rest are youths who are no longer in school,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Malaysia rolls out real-time dashboard to track global supply crisis impact
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 21 — The Ministry of Economy together with the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) has launched a new digital platform to monitor how the global supply crisis is affecting the country’s economy in near real time. The initiative, known as the Global Supply Crisis Monitoring Dashboard, is a fully integrated system designed to track economic spillovers from global disruptions, including energy prices, trade flows and supply chain pressures, according to a joint announcement by Economy Ministry and Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM). The dashboard provides users with up-to-date economic indicators, including movements in the cost of living, fluctuations in commodity and global market prices, as well as analysis of Malaysia’s trade performance and broader economic trends.

items.title: Malaysia’s longest river bridge opens today as RM848.75m Batang Lupar link connects Sebuyau and Maludam
items.summary: 1 Bridge, linking Sebuyau and Maludam, which will open to traffic today, is the longest river-crossing bridge in Malaysia. The 1.7-kilometre-long Sungai Johor Bridge was previously the country’s longest river-crossing bridge. Spanning approximately 4.8 kilometres across the Batang Lupar, the new bridge forms part of Sarawak’s wider infrastructure transformation under the Coastal Road Network and Second Trunk Road Programme aimed at improving connectivity between coastal areas and major towns.

items.title: Trapped lorry driver found dead after premix‑laden truck overturns into muddy ditch in Sebuyau
items.summary: KUCHING, May 21 — A lorry driver was found dead after being trapped under mud following a self-accident involving a premix-laden lorry along Jalan Batang Lupar–Sadong near Kampung Tebeluk, Sebuyau today. According to a statement, police initial findings suggested the incident occurred around 5am after a front tyre burst, causing the driver to lose control before the lorry skidded into a muddy ditch. “The accident caused the driver to become trapped inside the lorry under a pile of mud,” the department said in a statement.

items.title: Selangor police bust four call centre syndicates, 43 suspects taken into custody
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 21 — Police arrested 43 individuals, including a local citizen, believed to be involved in four separate call centre syndicates following several raids around Shah Alam and Puchong on May 19 and yesterday. Selangor Police Chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said that through the arrests at a two-story bungalow in Setia Alam here on May 19, police detained 13 foreigners and a local citizen believed to be involved in a part-time job offer scam targeting Iraqi nationals. Meanwhile, Shazeli said police also arrested 29 foreigners, including two women, in three separate raids conducted between 11.00pm and 1.20am at Bandar 16 Sierra, Puchong, on May 19 and yesterday.

items.title: MCMC serves statutory demand to TikTok, cites failure to act on ‘offensive’ content on royals
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 21 — Malaysia’s communications regulator said today it has issued ‌a statutory demand to social media platform TikTok alleging it had failed to take sufficient and timely action in moderating what it called offensive and ‌defamatory content on the country’s monarchy. The action arose from the alleged circulation of content associated with an account purporting to be linked to Malaysia’s King Sultan Ibrahim, the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission said in a statement. The content included material that was “grossly offensive, false, menacing and insulting in nature”, including AI-generated videos and manipulated images, that may contravene local laws, the commission said.

items.title: AGC opposes Malaysian Bar’s bid to challenge NFA ruling in Zahid’s Yayasan Akalbudi graft case
items.summary: The matter was raised by federal counsel Nurhafizza Azizan, representing the AGC, during case management before High Court Judge Alice Loke Yee Ching. In a separate related case, Nurhafizza said the AGC would also file for leave to appeal at the Federal Court against a Court of Appeal decision on May 7 allowing the Malaysian Bar’s appeal. Lawyer Mohamed Shahrul Fazli Kamarulzaman, representing Ahmad Zahid, said they would also file a similar application at the Federal Court.

items.title: Man found dead with knife in hand and multiple stab wounds in Kota Bharu bathroom
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 21 — A 28‑year‑old man was found dead with multiple stab wounds at a house on Jalan Masjid Baung Bayam in Kota Bharu yesterday. Kota Bharu district police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Azmir Damiri said the victim was found with a knife in his hand, adding that “there were two stab wounds on the chin, one on the neck and a small stab wound on the victim’s abdomen.” He said the victim’s aunt had entered the bathroom and saw blood on the floor before finding the man covered in blood with the knife still in his hand. Mohd Azmir said the victim had no known medical history but had been involved in drug abuse over the past year while working in Johor.

items.title: Rescue underway after soil‑laden lorry overturns in Sebuyau, driver believed trapped in mud
items.summary: KUCHING, May 21 — A lorry driver is believed to be buried under mud following a single-vehicle accident involving a soil-laden lorry along Jalan Batang Lupar-Sadong in Sebuyau this morning. The Fire and Rescue Department (Bomba) said it received an emergency call regarding the incident at 7.06am and deployed firefighters from the Asajaya fire station to the scene. Upon arrival, it was found that the accident involved a lorry carrying soil, which had overturned and caused the driver to become trapped inside the mud-filled vehicle.

items.title: Steamboat restaurant chain director claims trial to CBT charge over RM1.4m misappropriation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 21 — A director of a steamboat restaurant chain pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court here today to a charge of criminal breach of trust (CBT) involving more than RM1.4 million in company funds two years ago. Yong Pitt Lok, 48, was charged, in his capacity as a person entrusted with control over the company’s funds, with committing CBT involving RM1,468,958.29, which was allegedly transferred into his personal bank account for his own benefit. Deputy public prosecutor Muhammad Zul Hilmi Latiff proposed bail at RM120,000 in one surety.

items.title: Police reveal former lecturer scammed of RM749,000 after suspects claimed money laundering link
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, May 21 — A former lecturer lost RM749,000 after she was deceived by a phone scam last year, Kuala Terengganu district police chief ACP Azli Mohd Noor said. He added that the victim was contacted on July 13, 2025 by suspects impersonating insurance agents and the police, who accused her of being involved in money laundering and had to pay a sum of money for a Bank Negara investigation. She was also instructed to transfer money to several different accounts they provided.

items.title: US denies triggering Norway’s export halt, says it helped integrate missile components for Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 21 — The United States has denied any involvement in Norway’s decision to revoke export approval for a naval strike missile system ordered by Malaysia. The clarification came after reports by Free Malaysia Today and other outlets highlighted Norway’s move to cancel the export permit for the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) system supplied by Kongsberg Defence & Aerospace under a 2018 contract. US embassy chargé d’affaires David H Gamble Jr said Washington supported Malaysia’s efforts to strengthen its maritime defence, adding that the US backed Malaysia’s acquisition of a naval strike capability to respond to regional threats and to protect its sovereignty.

items.title: EC gazettes April voter roll, with 39,111 Malaysians automatically registered under Undi18
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 21 — A total of 39,111 citizens aged 18 and above between April 1 and 30 were automatically registered as new voters under the April 2026 Supplementary Electoral Roll (DPT BLN4/2026). Election Commission (EC) secretary Datuk Khairul Shahril Idrus said the supplementary electoral roll, which was verified on May 20 and gazetted today, also contained 15,880 registered voters who changed constituencies and 1,369 voters who changed voter categories or status. Khairul Shahril said voters whose names are not listed in DPT BLN4/2026 may submit claims by filling Form C through the EC online application or at the relevant state election office.

items.title: PM Anwar pledges commitment to secure release of Gaza flotilla activists, says SNCC
items.summary: SEPANG, May 21 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has given his assurance and full commitment to lead efforts through bilateral ties to secure the immediate release of Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) 2.0 humanitarian activists detained by Israel. Sumud Nusantara Command Centre (SNCC) director-general Datuk Sani Araby Abdul Alim Araby said the commitment was conveyed by the prime minister to SNCC during a telephone conversation today. “They also condemned the interception and detention of humanitarian flotilla activists in international waters,” he said.

items.title: Tengku Zafrul thanks Selangor Sultan for Aidiladha reminder on unity and Rukun Negara
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 21 — Senior political advisor to the Prime Minister Tengku Datuk Seri Zafrul Tengku Abdul Aziz has expressed his gratitude to the Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, for granting him an audience yesterday and for His Royal Highness’ reminder on the importance of unity. In a Facebook post, Tengku Zafrul said the Sultan’s advice should be understood within the framework of Malaysia’s constitutional monarchy, which has long been a pillar of national stability. Tengku Zafrul stressed that the Sultan’s call was aimed at strengthening unity across Malaysia’s diverse society, not solely among Malays, and highlighted how Malay and Islamic values should serve as a foundation for national stability that protects all communities.

items.title: Retiree, 75, pleads not guilty to reckless driving charge in Klang crash that killed two sisters
items.summary: KLANG, May 21 — A 75-year-old man pleaded not guilty at the Magistrate’s Court here today to a charge of reckless driving that caused the deaths of two sisters, last Wednesday. Ang Kim Lai made the plea after the charge was read to him in Mandarin before Magistrate A. Khartiyayini. According to the charge, Ang, a private sector retiree, who was driving a sport utility vehicle (SUV), was accused of driving recklessly and causing the deaths of Wong Nyok Sim, 62, and Wong Nyoke Fong, 57, at Jalan Pekan Baru here at 7.30pm on May 15.

items.title: Amirudin lauds Selangor Sultan’s Aidiladha message urging Malaysians to defend unity, uphold Rukun Negara
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 21 — Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari today expressed his gratitude for the Hari Raya Aidiladha 2026 address by the Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah. He said the Sultan’s message highlighted the importance of unity, harmony in Malaysia’s multiracial society, and the duty of all citizens to safeguard peace and stability in Selangor and nationwide. Amirudin noted the royal address came at a time when some quarters were exploiting racial and religious sentiments and politicising the monarchy for personal gain.

items.title: ‘Everything remains under control’: Penang’s essential goods supply stable with no sharp price increases, says CM
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 21 — The supply of essential goods in Penang is stable with no significant hike in prices, according to chief minister Chow Kon Yeow. “The 27 price-controlled items are in sufficient supply and are being sold below the controlled price,” he said in a press conference after visiting a local fruits and vegetables campaign at Komtar today. Everything remains under control,” he said.

items.title: Tony Pua faces 59 police reports nationwide over allegedly sensitive Facebook remarks on monarchy, Rukun Negara
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 21 — A total of 59 police reports have been lodged nationwide against former Damansara MP Tony Pua following a Facebook post alleged to have raised sensitive issues involving Malaysia’s constitutional monarchy and the Rukun Negara. Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said the investigation will be carried out by Selangor police, and Pua will be called in soon, according to Bernama. “As of this morning, I was briefed that 59 reports have been filed nationwide, with investigations led by the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) Selangor,” he told reporters at a press conference at the Selangor police headquarters today.

items.title: Too few Malaysians getting flu shots despite high awareness, doctors warn ahead of mid-year influenza season
items.summary: The call comes ahead of the country’s mid-year influenza season, with medical groups cautioning that older adults and those with chronic diseases remain vulnerable to severe complications from influenza infections. Of that group, only about half said they received the influenza shot annually. The survey was conducted collaboratively by the Health Ministry, the Malaysian Influenza Working Group (MIWG), and several public universities.

items.title: Babysitter remanded six days over death of seven‑month‑old boy in Seremban
items.summary: SEREMBAN, May 21 — A babysitter detained to assist investigations into the death of a seven-month-old baby boy at a house in Bandar Sri Sendayan near here last Tuesday has been remanded for six days until May 25. Meanwhile, Negeri Sembilan Social Welfare Department (JKM) director Muhammad Yusry Che Dah said investigations found that the childcare centre was unregistered and operating without approval under the Child Care Centre Act 1984. Most cases involving the death or injury of children occur at unregistered babysitting homes and childcare centres,” he said.

items.title: Motorcyclist trapped and killed after rear‑ending excavator at Federal Highway exit in Shah Alam
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 21 — A motorcyclist was killed after being trapped beneath an excavator in a road accident at the Federal Highway exit towards the junction of a sugar factory at midnight last night. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ahmad Mukhlis Mokhtar said a team of five firemen in an engine from the Shah Alam station were deployed to the scene after they received a call at 12.06 am. “The victim, who was trapped beneath the excavator, was pronounced dead by Health Ministry personnel.

items.title: Motorcyclist dies at scene after suspected collision with cow in Tapah
items.summary: Tapah police chief Supt Abdul Malik Hashim said initial investigations found that the victim, a man in his 20s, was riding alone and was on his way home to Sungai Manik after work when the incident occurred. “Investigations at the scene found traces of cow dung and skid marks on the road. Abdul Malik said the case is being investigated under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987.

items.title: Penang police bust ‘Piau Piau’ vape‑drug syndicate, arrest two and seize RM199,000 in drugs and assets
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 21 — Drugs packed into vape cartridges and sold on the streets as “Piau Piau” have resurfaced in Penang, after police busted a syndicate and seized almost RM200,000 worth of drugs and assets. He said the syndicate was found to be distributing a mix of methamphetamine liquid, MDMA powder, ketamine and Erimin 5 pills, with drugs concealed in vape‑style cartridges. He said the seizure included 123 cartridges suspected to contain methamphetamine liquid weighing 1,348.36 g, 11 packets of MDMA powder weighing 396 g, three packets of ketamine weighing 2.55 g, and 70 Erimin 5 pills weighing 21.37 g, alongside five electronic devices.
